name: Vortex
summary: A canvas-based particle vortex with 3D simplex noise-driven flow fields and hue-cycling particles.
detailedDescription: Vortex creates a mesmerizing particle system using canvas rendering with 3D simplex noise for organic motion. The component initializes 700 particles (configurable) with properties stored in a Float32Array for performance. Each particle has position, velocity, life, TTL, speed, radius, and hue values. Motion is driven by 3D noise sampling at each particle's position with time-based z-offset, creating flowing vortex patterns. Particles move according to noise-derived angles (cos/sin), with velocity lerped toward noise direction at 0.5 factor for smooth turns. The color system uses configurable base hue (default 220, blue) with 100-unit range for variation, cycling through HSL space. Particles spawn across canvas width with y-variation (default ±100px from center), live for 50-200 frames, and have radius 1-3px. The render loop clears with configurable background color, draws particles with fade-in/out lifecycle, applies glow effects, and renders to screen. Animation runs via requestAnimationFrame with continuous noise evaluation. This creates a hypnotic, fluid particle vortex suitable for hero backgrounds, music visualizations, or any context requiring organic, flowing motion.
purpose: Generate organic particle flow with noise-driven motion
Create hypnotic vortex backgrounds with hue cycling
behavior: Initializes particles with random positions and properties
Updates particle velocity using 3D simplex noise
Lerps velocity toward noise direction for smooth flow
Cycles particle hues through configurable range
Manages particle lifecycle with fade in/out
Renders with glow effects via canvas filters
visualCharacteristics: Flowing particle streams driven by noise
recommendations: Best for premium landing page sections and hero compositions.
Use as a focal animated accent and pair with calmer surrounding components.
Use as a supporting atmospheric layer rather than the primary layout structure.
